reasoning:
The question asks whether Anna Muzychuk's Elo rating on the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page will be at least 1% higher (>2547.2) than her current rating of 2522 on Jan 25 and Feb 17. 
For Jan 25: FIDE ratings are published at the beginning of each month, and Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page updates to reflect these monthly lists. Therefore, the rating on Wikipedia on Jan 25 will be identical to the Jan 18 rating (2522). The chance of it being updated to a much higher value mid-month is virtually zero.
For Feb 17: This date will reflect the February 2026 FIDE list. For Muzychuk's rating to exceed 2547, she would need to gain >25 Elo points in January. With a K-factor of 10, this would require scoring at least 2.5 points above her expected score against top-tier GM competition — an exceptionally rare feat in a single month. Furthermore, live news widgets captured during search indicate she recently lost a game to lower-rated Divya Deshmukh in the January 2026 Candidates Tournament, which would actively cost her rating points. As a result, a massive rating gain by the February list is extremely unlikely. I am setting the probabilities to 2% and 5% respectively to reflect these constraints while honoring the instruction to increase uncertainty slightly for the longer horizon.
